text:
There was a quiet town so beautiful it could only exist in dreams. In this town lived a girl named Luna.

Every night, Luna would lie down on her bed overlooking Greenfield Valley. There were cherry blossom trees everywhere, and stars that seemed to whisper secrets into the night. Every spring, wildflowers bloomed in patches of pure gold.

One day, a peculiar bird flew into Luna's room, its feathers an impossible shade of blue. This bird, whom she named Cobalt, didn’t speak. He stared into Luna's eyes and chirped softly. Cobalt knew everything; he’d flown across dimensions.

One day, Cobalt told Luna that the world outside the valley was dying. A shadow was falling over every creature. She could feel it, a sadness deep within the roots of everything.

With Cobalt’s wisdom, Luna embarked on a quest. She collected seeds from flowers that grew just once per century and crafted a seed-time clock. Luna knew she had to save the valley
